title: Loss Functions
description: A ground-up tour of all 20 PyTorch loss functions — regression, classification, distribution, ranking, embedding, and metric learning — with equation breakdowns, derivations from probability theory, and side-by-side PyTorch and TensorFlow implementations.

readings.title: What Is a Loss Function?
readings.description: Training objectives, reduction modes, and how gradients flow from loss to weights.

readings.title: Regression Losses: L1, MSE, Huber, SmoothL1
readings.description: Point-estimate regression losses — their formulas, gradient shapes, and robustness to outliers.

readings.title: Probabilistic Regression: Poisson & Gaussian NLL
readings.description: Deriving loss functions from negative log-likelihood when the target follows a Poisson or Gaussian distribution.

readings.title: Classification Losses: BCE, CrossEntropy, NLL
readings.description: Binary and multi-class classification losses derived from information theory and maximum likelihood estimation.

readings.title: Multi-label & Margin Losses
readings.description: Hinge-based and multi-label losses: SoftMargin, MultiLabelSoftMargin, MultiMarginLoss, and MultiLabelMarginLoss.

readings.title: Distribution & Similarity Losses
readings.description: KL divergence, margin ranking, hinge embedding, and cosine embedding losses.

readings.title: Metric Learning: Triplet Losses
readings.description: TripletMarginLoss and TripletMarginWithDistanceLoss — learning embedding spaces where similar inputs cluster together.

labs.title: Loss Functions in PyTorch
labs.description: Implement, verify, and compare all major PyTorch loss functions from scratch.

labs.title: Loss Functions in TensorFlow
labs.description: Implement the same loss functions using TensorFlow/Keras, with custom training loops and GradientTape.
